evidence_quotes.quote: The "end-of-life plan" ensures that a game remains in a playable state even after the publisher/platform stops supporting it.

paragraphs.text: As a European citizen and as a game owner, I would like to be able to play the games I bought (which were sold as "goods") after an arbitrary amount of time.This entails that games should have an "end-of-life plan", as stated in the final version of the European Citizens Initiative named "Stop Killing Games". To reiterate, the "end-of-life plan" ensures that a game remains in a playable state even after the publisher/platform stops supporting it. Please consider adding the requirement of "end-of-life plans" into videogames as over a million of persons already signed in the European Citizens Initiative "Stop Killing Games". Thank you so much for taking the time to read this.
